title: HTML Emoji
content:
### HTML Emoji **Emojis** are characters from the Unicode character set. They display as small images. --- ### Emoji in HTML Simply copy-paste or use the Unicode number: ```html

I love coding! 😊

I love coding! 😊

``` --- ### Using UTF-8 Emojis work when your page uses UTF-8 encoding: ```html ``` --- ### Emoji by Unicode Number ```html

😀 - Grinning Face

😁 - Grinning Face with Smiling Eyes

😂 - Face with Tears of Joy

😄 - Smiling Face with Open Mouth

``` --- ### Common Emojis | Emoji | Code | Meaning | |-------|------|---------| | 😀 | `😀` | Grinning | | 😂 | `😂` | Tears of Joy | | ❤️ | `❤` | Heart | | 👍 | `👍` | Thumbs Up | | ✅ | `✅` | Check Mark | | ❌ | `❌` | Cross Mark | | ⭐ | `⭐` | Star | | 🔥 | `🔥` | Fire | | 👋 | `👋` | Wave | | 🎉 | `🎉` | Party Popper | --- ### Emoji Sizes Emojis inherit the font size: ```html

🔥 👍 🎉

``` --- > **Note:** Emoji display varies across platforms. Apple, Google, Microsoft, and Samsung each have their own designs. Most modern browsers support emojis.
codeExample:
Emojis by copy-paste: 😂 ❤️ 👍

Emojis by code: 😀 😂 ❤

🎉 Party Time! 🎉

👍 Like 👎 Dislike

quiz.question: What character encoding is needed for emojis to work?
